title: Capital Cities of the World — Training Manual
description: Comprehensive Desktop Procedure for learning world capital cities organized by region. This DTP covers identification, geographical mapping, and edge cases.

courses.title: European Capitals — Western Europe
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of Western European nations
courses.content:
Western European Capitals:
- France → Paris
- Germany → Berlin
- Spain → Madrid
- Portugal → Lisbon
- Italy → Rome
- Netherlands → Amsterdam (constitutional), The Hague (governmental)
- Belgium → Brussels
- Luxembourg → Luxembourg City
- Austria → Vienna
- Switzerland → Bern

Key Edge Case: The Netherlands has two capitals. Amsterdam is the constitutional capital, but The Hague is the seat of government.

courses.title: European Capitals — Eastern & Northern Europe
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of Eastern and Northern European nations
courses.content:
Eastern & Northern European Capitals:
- Poland → Warsaw
- Czech Republic → Prague
- Hungary → Budapest
- Romania → Bucharest
- Greece → Athens
- Sweden → Stockholm
- Norway → Oslo
- Finland → Helsinki
- Denmark → Copenhagen
- Iceland → Reykjavik

Key Facts: Budapest is actually two cities (Buda and Pest) united in 1873. Prague is one of the oldest capitals in Europe.

courses.title: Asian Capitals — East Asia
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of East Asian nations
courses.content:
East Asian Capitals:
- China → Beijing
- Japan → Tokyo
- South Korea → Seoul
- North Korea → Pyongyang
- Mongolia → Ulaanbaatar
- Taiwan → Taipei

Key Facts: Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world. Beijing means 'Northern Capital' in Mandarin.

courses.title: Asian Capitals — South & Southeast Asia
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of South and Southeast Asian nations
courses.content:
South & Southeast Asian Capitals:
- India → New Delhi
- Pakistan → Islamabad
- Bangladesh → Dhaka
- Sri Lanka → Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte (legislative), Colombo (commercial)
- Thailand → Bangkok
- Vietnam → Hanoi
- Philippines → Manila
- Indonesia → Jakarta (moving to Nusantara)
- Malaysia → Kuala Lumpur (official), Putrajaya (administrative)
- Myanmar → Naypyidaw
- Singapore → Singapore (city-state)

Key Edge Cases:
- Myanmar moved its capital from Yangon to Naypyidaw in 2006
- Indonesia is relocating its capital from Jakarta to Nusantara
- Malaysia has two capitals: KL is official, Putrajaya is administrative

courses.title: Americas — North & Central America
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of North and Central American nations
courses.content:
North & Central American Capitals:
- United States → Washington, D.C.
- Canada → Ottawa
- Mexico → Mexico City
- Guatemala → Guatemala City
- Cuba → Havana
- Panama → Panama City
- Costa Rica → San José
- Jamaica → Kingston

Key Facts: Many people incorrectly think New York or Toronto are capitals. Washington D.C. is a federal district, not a state.

courses.title: Americas — South America
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of South American nations
courses.content:
South American Capitals:
- Brazil → Brasília
- Argentina → Buenos Aires
- Colombia → Bogotá
- Peru → Lima
- Chile → Santiago
- Venezuela → Caracas
- Ecuador → Quito
- Bolivia → Sucre (constitutional), La Paz (governmental)
- Paraguay → Asunción
- Uruguay → Montevideo

Key Edge Case: Bolivia has two capitals — Sucre is the constitutional capital and seat of the judiciary, while La Paz is the seat of government and executive/legislative branches.

courses.title: African Capitals — North & West Africa
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of North and West African nations
courses.content:
North & West African Capitals:
- Egypt → Cairo
- Morocco → Rabat
- Algeria → Algiers
- Tunisia → Tunis
- Libya → Tripoli
- Nigeria → Abuja
- Ghana → Accra
- Senegal → Dakar
- Ivory Coast → Yamoussoukro (official), Abidjan (economic)

Key Facts: Nigeria moved its capital from Lagos to Abuja in 1991. Many people still think Lagos is the capital.

courses.title: African Capitals — East & Southern Africa
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of East and Southern African nations
courses.content:
East & Southern African Capitals:
- Kenya → Nairobi
- Ethiopia → Addis Ababa
- Tanzania → Dodoma (official), Dar es Salaam (former, largest city)
- South Africa → Pretoria (executive), Bloemfontein (judicial), Cape Town (legislative)
- Zimbabwe → Harare
- Mozambique → Maputo
- Madagascar → Antananarivo

Key Edge Case: South Africa has THREE capitals — Pretoria (executive), Bloemfontein (judicial), and Cape Town (legislative). This is the most complex multi-capital arrangement in the world.

courses.title: Middle East & Oceania
courses.description: Learn the capital cities of Middle Eastern and Oceanian nations
courses.content:
Middle Eastern Capitals:
- Turkey → Ankara
- Saudi Arabia → Riyadh
- UAE → Abu Dhabi
- Israel → Jerusalem (declared), Tel Aviv (international recognition)
- Iraq → Baghdad
- Iran → Tehran

Oceanian Capitals:
- Australia → Canberra
- New Zealand → Wellington
- Fiji → Suva
- Papua New Guinea → Port Moresby

Key Facts: Many people think Sydney is Australia's capital (it's Canberra). Istanbul is Turkey's largest city but Ankara is the capital.

courses.title: Comprehensive Review & Edge Cases
courses.description: Review all regions and master the tricky edge cases — countries with multiple capitals, recent capital changes, and common misconceptions
courses.content:
EDGE CASES — Countries with Multiple Capitals:
1. South Africa: Pretoria (executive), Bloemfontein (judicial), Cape Town (legislative)
2. Bolivia: Sucre (constitutional), La Paz (governmental)
3. Malaysia: Kuala Lumpur (official), Putrajaya (administrative)
4. Netherlands: Amsterdam (constitutional), The Hague (governmental)
5. Sri Lanka: Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte (legislative), Colombo (commercial)
6. Ivory Coast: Yamoussoukro (official), Abidjan (economic)
7. Tanzania: Dodoma (official), Dar es Salaam (former)

RECENT CAPITAL CHANGES:
- Myanmar: Yangon → Naypyidaw (2006)
- Nigeria: Lagos → Abuja (1991)
- Brazil: Rio de Janeiro → Brasília (1960)
- Indonesia: Jakarta → Nusantara (ongoing)

COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS:
- New York is NOT the capital of the USA (Washington, D.C.)
- Sydney is NOT the capital of Australia (Canberra)
- Istanbul is NOT the capital of Turkey (Ankara)
- Toronto is NOT the capital of Canada (Ottawa)
- São Paulo is NOT the capital of Brazil (Brasília)
- Lagos is NOT the capital of Nigeria (Abuja)

key_facts.topic: Common mistake: New York ≠ USA's capital
